message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state from concurrent agent updates

Beads issue metadata refreshed by parallel agents — minor status and
field updates across tracked entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1e7k","title":"fix: hash collision prevention and sequence monotonicity in divergence_gate and approval_workflow","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:11:15.230069442Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-07T15:11:47.249052594Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T15:11:47.248942138Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1e8e3","title":"Audit remediation: hard-coded constants, stub implementations, and unfinished code paths","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:02:17.517803454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T07:33:40.779360160Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T07:33:40.779337828Z","close_reason":"All 3 tracks completed. Track 1: 4 security stubs fixed (Ed25519, rollback proof, context fingerprint). Track 2: 8 functional stubs fixed. Track 3: 8 config/centralization tasks done.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1e8e3","depends_on_id":"bd-1mdgw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1e8e3","depends_on_id":"bd-1wb0c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1e8e3","depends_on_id":"bd-2tj88","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":18,"issue_id":"bd-1e8e3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"MASTER EPIC — Comprehensive audit of the entire franken_node codebase (2026-03-24) identified three classes of issues:

Track 1: Security-Critical Stubs (P1) — 4 production code paths use fake/placeholder cryptographic or verification logic. The two signature-verification stubs (impossible_default.rs, threshold_sig.rs) use hash-based signatures that anyone can forge. The rollback proof and context fingerprint stubs undermine audit/replay integrity guarantees. These are the highest priority because they affect security invariants the rest of the system depends on.

Track 2: Functional/Audit Stubs (P2) — 6 production code paths have placeholder logic with explicit in-production-this-would comments. Includes missing audit events (CAN-006 leak detection), no-op executors (activation pipeline), hardcoded placeholder values (history_depth=0.7, duration_ms=0), and simplified implementations (mitigation synthesis uses signum*-5 heuristic). These affect operational correctness and observability but do not create direct security vulnerabilities.

Track 3: Configuration and Centralization (P2-P3) — ~150+ hard-coded capacity constants (mostly 4096), ~10 unconfigurable timeout/TTL values, ~12 algorithmic thresholds, ~15 scattered schema version strings, 2 hard-coded absolute paths, and 1 hard-coded timestamp anchor. These create scaling limitations, deployment brittleness, and maintenance burden. The fix is infrastructure: centralize constants, wire into config, expose via CLI/env.

Discovery methodology: Full-codebase grep for TODO/FIXME/HACK/will/would/placeholder/stub/not-yet/eventually plus systematic search for const declarations, magic numbers, hard-coded strings, absolute paths, timestamps, URLs. Each finding was manually triaged by reading surrounding code to distinguish genuine stubs from explanatory comments. 8 of 18 would-comments were confirmed as genuine stubs; the other 10 are explanatory comments about intentionally in-memory/synchronous designs.

Relationship to prior work: This builds on the 650+ bugs fixed across sessions 2026-02-22 through 2026-03-19. Those sessions focused on correctness bugs (overflow, timing, boundary, path traversal). This audit targets a different class: architectural completeness and configurability.
","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1e8sf","title":"Track 2d: Graceful runtime fallback — use node/bun when franken-engine is missing","notes":"PLATFORM DETAIL: Runtime discovery must use which::which crate for cross-platform binary resolution instead of raw PATH search. Test on Linux (primary) with node/bun in various PATH locations. The --runtime flag should accept: auto (default, try franken-engine then node then bun), node, bun, franken-engine. If explicit runtime requested and not found, exit with code 127 (command not found convention) and structured error. Add which = 7 to dev-dependencies.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:10:08.855992896Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T14:21:54.095886902Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-1eaqy","title":"Program: close all reality-check gaps — make franken_node deliver on every README promise","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:08:27.550681876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T14:21:05.116216343Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1eaqy","depends_on_id":"bd-2e56f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:14:02.315156551Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1eaqy","depends_on_id":"bd-2n1mk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:14:01.831741689Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1eaqy","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2x8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:14:02.074811898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1eaqy","depends_on_id":"bd-770ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T14:21:05.116155008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1eaqy","depends_on_id":"bd-kyvjq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:14:02.854532098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-1eaqy","title":"Program: close all reality-check gaps — make franken_node deliver on every README promise","notes":"HARDENING: All new code must follow the established hardening patterns documented in MEMORY.md:
- saturating_add() for ALL counter/sequence arithmetic
- ct_eq()/ct_eq_bytes() for ALL signature/hash comparisons
- push_bounded() for ALL struct-field Vec pushes
- is_finite() guards on f64 before arithmetic
- Path traversal validation (reject .., leading /, backslashes)
- Length-prefixed hash inputs for variable-length fields
- TempFileGuard RAII with defuse() for atomic rename patterns
- b"module_function_v1:" domain separators in all hashers

DOCUMENTATION: bd-2n1mk (README alignment) should also update AGENTS.md Architecture Reference section to reflect new fleet transport, runtime enforcement, and registry infrastructure.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:08:27.550681876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T14:25:38.168511194Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1eaqy","depends_on_id":"bd-2e56f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:14:02.315156551Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1eaqy","depends_on_id":"bd-2n1mk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:14:01.831741689Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1eaqy","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2x8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:14:02.074811898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1eaqy","depends_on_id":"bd-770ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T14:21:05.116155008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1eaqy","depends_on_id":"bd-kyvjq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:14:02.854532098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1efb","title":"Eviction saga terminal cancellation and RemoteCap recheck hardening","notes":"Multi-agent split active (2026-02-25): CoralElk=remote/eviction_saga.rs, VioletCanyon=connector/eviction_saga.rs, ScarletGlacier=security/remote_cap.rs (recheck hardening + tests).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CoralElk","created_at":"2026-02-23T19:13:35.204316975Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T08:37:16.364204352Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T08:26:29.090673065Z","close_reason":"Completed split lanes; node check+clippy pass after cross-repo blocker clearance","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":19,"issue_id":"bd-1efb","author":"VioletCanyon","text":"Taking connector-side sub-scope to avoid overlap: reserved crates/franken-node/src/connector/eviction_saga.rs. Remote module is reserved by CoralElk; I released that path. Implementing terminal-cancellation + RemoteCap recheck hardening tests/code in connector module and will report results.","created_at":"2026-02-25T06:31:53Z"},{"id":20,"issue_id":"bd-1efb","author":"CoralElk","text":"Remote lane update: completed implementation in crates/franken-node/src/remote/eviction_saga.rs. Added ES_REMOTECAP_RECHECK event + recheck_remote_cap(...) API, enforced RemoteCap recheck before begin_upload/complete_upload/complete_verify, and hardened terminal cancellation via shared cancel guard. Added tests for recheck block/restore and terminal cancellation (Complete + Compensated). Validation: rch exec cargo check --all-targets PASSED; rch exec cargo clippy -p frankenengine-node --all-targets -D warnings PASSED; rch exec cargo test -p frankenengine-node remote::eviction_saga PASSED (29 tests). cargo fmt --check currently fails due unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in tests/integration/bayesian_risk_quarantine.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-25T06:49:57Z"},{"id":21,"issue_id":"bd-1efb","author":"ScarletGlacier","text":"Completed ScarletGlacier lane in crates/franken-node/src/security/remote_cap.rs: added recheck_network (non-consuming), internalized consume behavior, new REMOTECAP_RECHECK_PASSED audit event, and tests recheck_does_not_consume_single_use_token + recheck_honors_revocation. Validation via rch: check passed in-snapshot; clippy/test currently blocked by unrelated franken_engine compile failures (dual_backend_parser E0502, simd_lexer E0425); fmt --check also failing due pre-existing unrelated formatting drift.","created_at":"2026-02-25T07:04:21Z"},{"id":22,"issue_id":"bd-1efb","author":"ScarletGlacier","text":"Follow-up: fresh rch check now fails outside this lane in franken_engine/src/parser_multi_engine_harness.rs:686 (E0277, EngineOutcomeKind missing Ord for BTreeSet). Shared-state blocker; remote_cap lane unchanged.","created_at":"2026-02-25T07:07:03Z"},{"id":23,"issue_id":"bd-1efb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"VioletCanyon connector lane complete: updated connector/eviction_saga.rs with RemoteCap recheck enforcement on upload_complete/verify_complete/retire_complete, added ERR_ES_REMOTE_CAP_RECHECK_FAILED, hardened compensate() re-entry handling, and added/updated tests (remote cap recheck + compensating re-entry + signature updates). Validation via rch: cargo test --manifest-path crates/franken-node/Cargo.toml connector::eviction_saga::tests => PASS (39/39 in lib + 39/39 in main targets). cargo check --manifest-path crates/franken-node/Cargo.toml --all-targets => PASS (exit=0). cargo clippy -p frankenengine-node --all-targets -- -D warnings => BLOCKED by franken_engine compile errors in structural_causal_model.rs (E0308/pattern binding errors). cargo fmt --all --check => FAIL due pre-existing unrelated formatting drift in tests/integration/bayesian_risk_quarantine.rs; connector file itself now passes rustfmt --check.","created_at":"2026-02-25T07:17:50Z"},{"id":24,"issue_id":"bd-1efb","author":"ScarletGlacier","text":"Cross-repo blocker recheck complete (2026-02-25): rch-offloaded cargo check and cargo clippy for package frankenengine-node now pass with exit 0. Prior franken_engine compile blockers no longer reproduce in current snapshots. Extra targeted security::remote_cap::tests rerun was attempted but remote job stalled and was canceled to avoid queue purgatory; existing lane-level focused test evidence from CoralElk, VioletCanyon, and ScarletGlacier remains passing. From this lane: bead is ready for closure.","created_at":"2026-02-25T08:26:23Z"},{"id":25,"issue_id":"bd-1efb","author":"CoralElk","text":"Fresh-eyes pass on remote eviction saga complete. Added RemoteCap gate on complete_retire plus tests for complete_verify/complete_retire recheck blocking. No additional logic defects found in this lane. Validation attempts via rch hit infra issues (root sync stalls, worker /tmp ENOSPC, one worker missing workspace root in crate-scope sync); rustfmt check on edited file passes.","created_at":"2026-02-25T08:37:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1eot","title":"[10.19] Integrate privacy-preserving urgent routing for revocation/quarantine signals.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.19 — Adversarial Trust Commons Execution Track (9M)

Why This Exists:
ATC federated intelligence track for privacy-preserving cross-deployment threat learning, robust aggregation, and verifier-backed trust metrics.

Task Objective:
Integrate privacy-preserving urgent routing for revocation/quarantine signals.

Acceptance Criteria:
- High-severity signals propagate within policy SLO while preserving privacy envelopes; urgent route decisions are signed and replayable.

Expected Artifacts:
- `docs/specs/atc_urgent_signal_routing.md`, `tests/integration/atc_urgent_routing_latency.rs`, `artifacts/10.19/atc_urgent_routing_telemetry.csv`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_19/bd-1eot/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_19/bd-1eot/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.19] Integrate privacy-preserving urgent routing for revocation/quarantine signals.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.19] Integrate privacy-preserving urgent routing for revocation/quarantine signals." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.19] Integrate privacy-preserving urgent routing for revocation/quarantine signals." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.19] Integrate privacy-preserving urgent routing for revocation/quarantine signals." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.19] Integrate privacy-preserving urgent routing for revocation/quarantine signals." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"- High-severity signals propagate within policy SLO while preserving privacy envelopes; urgent route decisions are signed and replayable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:06.087676863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T07:07:28.991509826Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T07:07:28.991482004Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-19","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1eot","depends_on_id":"bd-ukh7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3enl","title":"[10.3] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.3

Task Objective:
Create a hard section completion gate that verifies all implemented behavior in this section with comprehensive unit tests, integration/e2e scripts, and detailed structured logging evidence.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Section test matrix covers happy-path, edge-case, and adversarial/error-path scenarios for all section deliverables.
- E2E scripts execute representative end-user workflows and policy/control-plane transitions for this section.
- Structured logs include stable event/error codes, trace correlation IDs, and enough context for deterministic replay triage.
- Verification report is deterministic and machine-readable for CI/release gating.

Expected Artifacts:
- Section-level test matrix and coverage summary artifact.
- E2E script suite with pass/fail outputs and reproducible fixtures/seeds.
- Structured log validation report and traceability bundle.
- Gate verdict artifact consumable by release automation.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_3/bd-3enl/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_3/bd-3enl/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests must validate contracts, invariants, and failure semantics.
- E2E tests must validate cross-component behavior from user/operator entrypoints to persisted effects.
- Logging must support root-cause analysis without hidden context requirements.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.3] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.3] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging" must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.3] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging" must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.3] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.3] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","notes":"Root-cause addendum: this section gate must explicitly validate the bd-2avo replacement work with shadow -> canary -> ramp -> default rollout E2E flows, degraded-evidence scenarios, signed migration artifact integrity, minimal counterexample witness emission, and structured MIGRATION_* logs. Require at least one deterministic shell E2E script under tests/e2e/ plus evidence artifacts that record the rch build or test IDs used for  {"id":"bd-3ex","title":"[10.7] Add verifier CLI conformance contract tests.","description":"## [10.7] Verifier CLI Conformance Contract Tests

### Why This Exists

The verifier CLI (`franken-node verify`) is a public interface used by external verifiers, CI pipelines, and the verifier economy (10.17, 10.12). Its behavior must be stable and predictable across versions — breaking changes to input formats, output schemas, exit codes, or error messages would break downstream consumers silently. This bead establishes contract tests that pin the CLI's observable behavior, detect unintended breaking changes, and require explicit version bumps when intentional breaking changes are made.

### What It Must Do

**Contract definition**: Define the verifier CLI's contract in a machine-readable format (`spec/verifier_cli_contract.toml` or similar). The contract specifies: (1) accepted input formats (file paths, stdin, glob patterns), (2) output JSON schema for success and error cases, (3) exit codes and their meanings (0 = all checks pass, 1 = some checks fail, 2 = invalid input, 3 = internal error), (4) error message format (structured JSON with error code, human message, and remediation hint), and (5) command-line flag inventory with types and defaults.

**Contract test suite**: A test suite that exercises every aspect of the contract with specific, pinned inputs and expected outputs. Tests use snapshot-style assertions — the expected output is stored alongside the test and compared byte-for-byte (after normalization of timestamps and paths). Adding a new flag or output field is non-breaking; removing or changing one is breaking.

**Breaking change detection**: When a contract test fails, the failure message clearly distinguishes between "new field added" (non-breaking, auto-update snapshot) and "existing field changed/removed" (breaking, requires version bump). A `--update-snapshots` flag regenerates expected outputs for non-breaking changes.

**Version enforcement**: The CLI embeds a contract version (semver). Breaking changes increment the major version. The contract test suite validates that the embedded version matches the contract definition's version. If a breaking change is detected without a version bump, the gate fails.

**Backward compatibility layer**: When a major version bump occurs, the CLI supports `--compat-version=<N>` to produce output in the previous format for one major version back, giving consumers time to migrate.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. Verifier CLI contract is defined in a machine-readable file specifying input formats, output schemas, exit codes, error formats, and flag inventory.
2. Contract test suite covers every exit code, every output field, every error code, and every accepted input format with pinned expected outputs.
3. Breaking vs. non-breaking change detection works: new fields pass, changed/removed fields fail with clear breaking-change message.
4. Contract version is embedded in CLI output and validated against the contract definition.
5. `--update-snapshots` regenerates expected outputs for non-breaking changes only.
6. `--compat-version=<N>` produces output in the previous major version's format.
7. Verification script `scripts/check_verifier_contract.py` with `--json` flag validates the contract test suite.
8. Unit tests in `tests/test_check_verifier_contract.py` cover contract parsing, snapshot comparison, breaking change detection, version validation, and compat-version output.

### Key Dependencies

- Verifier CLI implementation (`franken-node verify` subcommand).
- CLI framework from bd-n9r (cli.rs).
- JSON Schema for output validation.
- Existing verification scripts that the CLI orchestrates.

### Testing & Logging Requirements

- Stability test: run contract tests against current CLI, assert all pass.
- Breaking change test: modify an output field, run tests, assert breaking-change failure.
- Non-breaking change test: add a new output field, run tests, assert pass (or auto-update).
- Compat-version test: request previous version output, assert format matches previous contract.
- Structured JSON logs for each contract test execution: test name, expected vs. actual, pass/fail, breaking/non-breaking classification.

### Expected Artifacts

- `spec/verifier_cli_contract.toml` — contract definition.
- `tests/contract/snapshots/` — pinned expected outputs.
- `scripts/check_verifier_contract.py` — verification script.
- `tests/test_check_verifier_contract.py` — unit tests.
- `artifacts/section_10_7/bd-3ex/verification_evidence.json` — gate results.
- `artifacts/section_10_7/bd-3ex/verification_summary.md` — human-readable summary.

### Additional E2E Requirement

- E2E test scripts must execute full end-to-end workflows from clean fixtures, emit deterministic machine-readable pass/fail evidence, and capture stepwise structured logs for root-cause triage.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Verifier CLI exposes at least: verify-module, verify-migration, verify-compatibility, and verify-corpus subcommands.
2. Each subcommand has a conformance contract defined in a spec document under docs/specs/ specifying inputs, outputs, exit codes, and error formats.
3. Contract tests exercise every specified input/output combination including edge cases and error paths.
4. Tests validate that CLI output conforms to the documented JSON schema (no undocumented fields, no missing required fields).
5. Exit codes follow a documented taxonomy: 0=pass, 1=fail, 2=error, 3=skipped.
6. Per Section 3.2 capability #10 (public verifier toolkit): CLI is usable by an external party with no internal knowledge — contract tests verify this by running in an isolated environment with no access to internal state.
7. Contract tests are generated from the spec documents (not hand-written) to ensure spec and tests stay in sync.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:47.589413741Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T02:58:30.655388646Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T02:58:30.655352739Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-7","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ex","depends_on_id":"bd-2ja","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3vyd4","title":"Fail closed when compatibility scope predicate set is at capacity","description":"Fresh audit: CompatGateEvaluator::add_policy_predicate() in crates/franken-node/src/policy/compat_gates.rs currently uses push_bounded(&mut scope.policy_predicates, predicate, MAX_ENTRIES). When a scope already has MAX_ENTRIES signed predicates, registering one more silently evicts the oldest predicate instead of failing. That can drop an active compatibility predicate from evaluation order, widen effective policy for the scope, and mutate signed-policy state without an explicit operator decision. Fix by failing closed at capacity before mutating scope.policy_predicates or the predicate caches, and add a focused regression proving overflow is rejected, the oldest predicate remains present, and the overflow predicate does not leak into compiled/validated caches.","notes":"Source patch landed in crates/franken-node/src/policy/compat_gates.rs. CompatGateEvaluator::add_policy_predicate() now rejects overflow before mutating scope.policy_predicates or predicate caches, and regression add_policy_predicate_rejects_overflow_without_eviction proves overflow should preserve the oldest predicate and avoid cache leakage. File-scoped rustfmt --edition 2024 and git diff --check are green. Two focused rch runs without extended-surfaces succeeded but executed zero tests because policy is feature-gated in crates/franken-node/src/lib.rs.
